revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:肥前国風土記]][[zh:肥前國風土記]]
'''Hizen no Kuni Fudoki''' (肥前国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|qid=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}) compiled during the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. It is one of the five surviving Fudoki.
== Compilation Period == The compilation date is believed to be between {{ill|Tenpyō 4|ja|天平4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (732), when the [[Chōri system]] was established as an administrative division, and Tenpyō 12 (740), when the Chōri system was abolished. This conclusion is supported by the detailed references to military infrastructure (castles and [[:en:beacons|beacons]]) in the text. There is no definitive evidence for this, but many scholars suggest that {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|qid=Q1370638|lt=Fujiwara no Umakai|1=Fujiwara no Umakai}}, after being appointed to {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|qid=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu}}, led the simultaneous compilation of Fudoki across the provinces under its jurisdiction. The similarities with the {{ill|Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|ja|豊後国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} strengthen this hypothesis.
== Overview == The origins of the province and district names, which are the central themes of most Fudoki, are described only briefly. This has led to the theory that the surviving manuscript is not the original but a transcription of an excerpted version.
The text contains numerous legends associated with {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, as well as stories involving {{ill|Tsuchigumo|ja|土蜘蛛#史料に見える土蜘蛛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchigumo|1=Tsuchigumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2063089}} and female leaders, such as {{ill|Ōyamata no Onna|ja|大山田女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamata no Onna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayamata no Onna|ja|狭山田女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayamata no Onna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yaso no Onna|ja|八十女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso no Onna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hayokitsu-hime|ja|速来津姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayokitsu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://jyashin.net/evilshrine/gods/tsuchigumo_shrine/tsuchigumo_ancient_list.html#hizen Hizen no Kuni Fudoki]. ''Ancient Tsuchigumo List''</ref><ref>[https://hiroda.net/hiroda-history/mishima/ Mishima Shrine]. ''Hiroda Community Council''</ref> This abundance of such narratives distinguishes the Hizen no Kuni Fudoki from others.
Some descriptions reveal a strong influence from earlier sources like the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.
A manuscript once owned by the {{ill|Inokuma family (Urabe clan)|ja|猪熊家 (卜部氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokuma family (Urabe clan)|1=Inokuma family (Urabe clan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116156510}}, referred to as the "Inokuma Manuscript," is believed to have been transcribed in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} and is designated as a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure]].
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.f.waseda.jp/hisaot/hizenhudoki.html Images of the entire manuscript of Hizen no Kuni Fudoki] by Waseda University Faculty of Letters [http://www.f.waseda.jp/hisaot/index.html Takamatsu Ancient Japanese Poetry Research] * {{Cite book|title=Fudoki (Kadokawa Bunko)|year=1970|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|pages=215–235|author=Eirei Kojima (Editor)|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12268944/1/109}} * [http://jyashin.net/evilshrine/gods/tsuchigumo_shrine/tsuchigumo_ancient_list.html Ancient Tsuchigumo List]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|肥前国風土記|version=103045744|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:肥前国風土記]]
